meaning: n.鍐荤粨鎴愬啺,鍐版渤浣滅敤
examples: The problem with ascribing all tillites to glaciation, however, is that tillite deposits are widespread in Precambrian sediments.
An increasingly attractive theory holds that decreases in atmospheric carbon dioxide start the cooling trend that leads to glaciation.
These hills were formed by glaciation.
Several scientists pointed out that during most of the past 10, 000 years, the time period since the last full-blown ice age glaciation ended, global temperatures were warmer than today.
